mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier discounting and lower funding stress, which lifts equity valuations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when the policy rate has declined for at least two consecutive monthly observations.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 2-month FEDFUNDS decline, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates above the recent trend while FEDFUNDS remains low, real rates stay suppressed and policy is perceived as behind inflation, favoring risk assets over the next 5 trading days. The claim fails if inflation acceleration does not coincide with positive equity response.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.3% m/m while FEDFUNDS is below 3.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show positive 5-day SPX returns more often than 50%, or if the sign flips negative when FEDFUNDS is below 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening labor income prospects and higher recession probability, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised lower. The effect is strongest when unemployment is rising from an already low level.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of unemployment-rising windows, or if the rolling 3-year correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns is nonnegative.

mechanism_narrative: PAYEMS growth combined with a stable or falling UNRATE reflects genuine labor-market expansion rather than labor-supply slack, which supports consumption and profits and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. The mechanism fails when payroll gains occur alongside a rising unemployment rate.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS is above its 3-month average and UNRATE is flat or falling
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform unconditional 60-day returns by at least 2 percentage points, or if returns are not positive in a majority of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS indicates stronger consumer spending and revenue growth expectations, which should lift equity prices over the next 20 trading days through a higher earnings outlook. The signal is valid only when sales growth is broad-based rather than a one-off spike.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS increases above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered periods do not produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the mean forward return is below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO signals stronger industrial output and supply-side momentum, which should improve cyclical earnings expectations and support S&P 500 gains over the next 60 trading days. The effect should weaken if industrial production rises while demand indicators stall.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX returns are not higher following positive INDPRO surprises, or if rolling Rank IC is below 0.03 for any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A combined rise in HOUST and GDPC1 indicates synchronized housing and aggregate demand strength, which should translate into positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days. The claim is falsified if housing starts improve without any accompanying GDP stabilization.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST-GDPC1 condition fails to outperform either proxy alone, or if triggered observations do not show positive excess 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth slows and CPIAUCSL remains elevated, the market should interpret the combination as stagflation risk, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. The mechanism requires both weaker real activity and persistent inflation pressure.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth below its prior 2-quarter average while CPIAUCSL inflation stays above 3% y/y
failure_condition: Reject if triggered periods do not produce negative 60-day SPX returns at least 55% of the time, or if returns are positive despite simultaneous growth slowdown and elevated inflation.
